pr.title: Cleanup OS layout based on other projects, match some functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large Dolphin OS cleanup aligning header/file layout with other decomp projects while also converting several previously handwritten or stubby OS routines into C or cleaner inline assembly. The PR renamed the OS umbrella header from src/dolphin/os/os.h to src/dolphin/os.h, removed src/dolphin/os/forward.h, updated many includes, normalized Pad file casing to lowercase pad.c/pad.h/pad.s, moved/expanded OS typedefs and structs into concrete subsystem headers, and matched/cleaned functions in OSContext, OSAlarm, OSInterrupt, OSMutex, OSThread, OSTime, OSSerial/SI, CARD/EXI, and related headers.

key_files.why: Renamed from src/dolphin/os/os.h and turned into the main Dolphin OS umbrella header including OSAlarm, OSAlloc, OSArena, OSCache, OSContext, OSError, OSException, OSExi, OSFont, OSInterrupt, OSLink, OSMemory, OSMutex, OSReset, OSResetSW, OSSerial, OSSync, OSThread, and OSTime.
Deleted; forward declarations and shared OS types were moved into concrete subsystem headers or the new umbrella header.

Expanded substantially with OS_CONTEXT_* offset macros, context state constants, the OSContext typedef/struct, handler typedefs, and OS_EXCEPTION_SAVE_GPRS macro used by exception-handler inline assembly.
Cleaned OSSwitchFPUContext inline assembly to use named OS_CONTEXT_* access macros and made the handler static/internal with __OSException typing.
Simplified DecrementerExceptionHandler inline assembly by replacing long explicit register-save instructions with OS_EXCEPTION_SAVE_GPRS(context), and made the handler static.
Applied the same exception-save macro pattern for interrupt dispatch assembly.

Replaced large MWERKS inline assembly implementations of __OSCheckMutex, __OSCheckDeadLock, and __OSCheckMutexes with C implementations, and updated field names such as mutexQueue to queueMutex and lock to count.
Moved RunQueueBits, RunQueueHint, and Reschedule out of asm sbss into static C globals; cleaned scheduler helper names/signatures; replaced the old OSSerial_804D73DC symbol use with RunQueueHint.
Removed sbss definitions for RunQueueBits, OSSerial_804D73DC, and Reschedule after migrating them into C.
Replaced the MWERKS assembly version of OSTicksToCalendarTime with a C implementation using OSTime and calendar conversion helpers.
Moved/defined EXIChannel and related EXI enums/types in the subsystem header; several APIs were adjusted to use s32 channel parameters where that better matched codegen.
New private SI header containing SICallback, SITypeAndStatusCallback, SIPacket, and SI private API prototypes such as SITransfer and SIGetResponse.
Renamed from Pad.c to lowercase pad.c to match project/source layout conventions.
Renamed from Pad.h, switched to include dolphin/os.h, and added extern u32 __PADFixBits.
Updated object list for the Pad.c/Pad.s to pad.c/pad.s renames.

what_changed: Renamed the Dolphin OS umbrella include from <dolphin/os/os.h> to <dolphin/os.h> and updated many includes across Dolphin, Melee fighter/stage code, sysdolphin, CARD, DVD, GX, PAD, and OS files.
Deleted src/dolphin/os/forward.h and replaced forward-header dependencies with concrete headers or the new <dolphin/os.h> umbrella include.

Expanded OSContext.h from a minimal struct header into a central OS context definition header with register offset macros such as OS_CONTEXT_R0, OS_CONTEXT_CR, OS_CONTEXT_SRR0, OS_CONTEXT_GQR*, OS_CONTEXT_PSF*, state constants such as OS_CONTEXT_STATE_FPSAVED, and OS_EXCEPTION_SAVE_GPRS.
Cleaned exception handler inline assembly in OSAlarm.c, OSContext.c, and OSInterrupt.c to use named macros and typed register parameters instead of long address-commented raw instruction blocks.
Converted OSMutex diagnostic/checking routines from hand-written assembly or NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks into C: __OSCheckMutex(OSMutex*), __OSCheckDeadLock(OSThread*), and __OSCheckMutexes(OSThread*).
Renamed OSMutex field lock to count and OSThread-owned mutex list usage from mutexQueue to queueMutex in affected code.
Moved OSThread scheduler globals RunQueueBits, RunQueueHint, and Reschedule into static C declarations in OSThread.c and removed their sbss allocation from asm/dolphin/os/OSThread_data.s.
Replaced the unclear OSSerial_804D73DC scheduler flag symbol with the descriptive RunQueueHint in OSThread assembly comments/references.
Changed __OSReschedule from an inline-assembly implementation into a simple C implementation that early-returns when RunQueueHint is false and calls SelectThread(false) otherwise.

Converted OSTicksToCalendarTime from a large MWERKS assembly block to a C implementation using OSTime and date/time calculations; OSTime.h signatures were updated from raw u64/s64 to OSTime where appropriate.
Adjusted CARD and EXI-related channel signatures in several internal APIs from EXIChannel to s32 where the diff shows this was needed for matching, while defining EXIChannel enum values in OSExi.h.

Added src/dolphin/sipriv.h for SI private declarations and updated SI signatures such as SIGetResponse(s32 chan, void* data), SITransfer(..., SICallback callback, OSTime delay), and SIGetTypeAsync(s32 chan, SITypeAndStatusCallback callback).
Renamed Pad source/header/asm files from Pad.c, Pad.h, Pad.s to pad.c, pad.h, pad.s and updated obj_files.mk accordingly.
Added additional file associations to .vscode/settings.json so standard-library-looking headers such as cstddef, limits, type_traits, xmemory, xutility, exception, and compare are treated as C.
smart_moves: Used an umbrella <dolphin/os.h> layout matching other decomp projects, reducing reliance on fragile forward headers and making OS type ownership clearer.

Moved low-level OS context offsets into named macros in OSContext.h, letting inline assembly reference semantic names like OS_CONTEXT_SRR1(context) and OS_CONTEXT_R3(context) instead of hard-coded offsets.
Collapsed repeated register-save instruction sequences into OS_EXCEPTION_SAVE_GPRS(context), improving maintainability while preserving the same exception-handler assembly shape.
Replaced opaque generated names such as OSSerial_804D73DC with domain meaning, RunQueueHint, after locating the variable’s scheduler role.
Migrated sbss symbols from standalone assembly into static C globals where the matching layout permitted it, shrinking asm data and making ownership/lifetime clearer.
Changed internal helper functions to static where they are file-local, such as DMAErrorHandler and exception/FPU handlers, reducing exported symbol noise.
Used exact public/private typedef placement instead of a catch-all forward header: OSAlarmHandler lives with OSAlarm, EXIChannel lives with OSExi, SI private callbacks live in sipriv.h.
When type-safe enum names hurt matching, the PR deliberately used s32 channel parameters in selected CARD/EXI/SI signatures, preserving ABI/codegen while still documenting enum values elsewhere.
decomp_lessons: For SDK/Dolphin library work, match the known SDK-style header layout first; cleaner type ownership can unlock many smaller matching fixes across unrelated systems.
Avoid central forward.h dumping grounds when subsystem headers can own their typedefs and callbacks; this makes include dependencies more predictable.
Inline assembly should use named offset macros for struct fields whenever possible. This keeps matching assembly readable and resilient to struct field renames.
A long handwritten assembly function may be matchable as C once struct fields and typedefs are accurate. OSMutex checks and OSTicksToCalendarTime are examples from this PR.
Do not assume enum-typed channel parameters always match. The PR changed several EXI/CARD/SI function parameters from EXIChannel to s32 where that matched codegen.
Promote meaningful names for unknown small-data symbols only when their role is proven by use sites; RunQueueHint is supported by its scheduler behavior and replacement of OSSerial_804D73DC.
File casing matters for build reproducibility and consistency. Pad.c/Pad.h/Pad.s were normalized to lowercase pad.c/pad.h/pad.s and obj_files.mk was updated with the rename.
Moving sbss variables from asm into C requires updating both the data assembly file and every asm/C reference to preserve small-data addressing and symbol order.
naming_conventions: Use <dolphin/os.h> as the OS umbrella include instead of <dolphin/os/os.h>.
Prefer lowercase Dolphin PAD file names in this tree: src/dolphin/pad/pad.c, src/dolphin/pad/pad.h, and asm/dolphin/pad/pad.s.
Use OS_CONTEXT_* uppercase macros for OSContext register offsets and fields referenced from assembly.
Use __OSException for internal exception-handler parameters where the PR changed handlers from OSException to __OSException.
Use OSMutex.count rather than OSMutex.lock for the recursive/acquisition count field.
Use OSThread.queueMutex for the thread-owned mutex queue, not mutexQueue.
Use RunQueueHint as the scheduler hint flag name instead of an unknown OSSerial_* placeholder.
Place SI private callbacks/types in src/dolphin/sipriv.h, including SICallback and SITypeAndStatusCallback.
Use typed time aliases in OSTime APIs, e.g. OSTime OSGetTime(void) and void OSTicksToCalendarTime(OSTime ticks, OSCalendarTime* td).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace explicit exception prologue stores with OS_EXCEPTION_SAVE_GPRS(context) to keep the same save sequence while making handlers readable.
Use register-qualified inline assembly parameters for handlers, e.g. register __OSException exception and register OSContext* context.
Use OS_CONTEXT_* access macros in inline assembly load/store operands instead of literal offsets like ctx->srr1 or hard-coded comments.
For FPU exception switching, preserve nofralloc/rfi structure while rewriting with named labels such as _loadNewFPUContext and _restoreAndExit.
C static globals can replace .sbss assembly allocations when symbol placement and small-data references are controlled; this PR did that for RunQueueBits, RunQueueHint, and Reschedule.
A tiny scheduler helper can match better as C than assembly: __OSReschedule became an if (!RunQueueHint) return; SelectThread(false); implementation.
Some function signatures were adjusted to match ABI/codegen rather than ideal semantic typing, notably changing selected EXIChannel parameters to s32.
OSTicksToCalendarTime was decompiled from a large assembly block into C by using OSTime, day/second decomposition, GetDates, and calendar-field assignments.

review_feedback.feedback: No substantive review comments were present in the PR slice. One review by ribbanya exists but has an empty body and was dismissed.

searchable_terms: Cleanup OS layout based on other projects
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue removing dolphin/os/forward.h dependencies or converting OS headers to SDK-style layout.
Search for uses of OS_EXCEPTION_SAVE_GPRS to see which exception handlers still have handwritten register-save blocks.
Search for remaining OSSerial_* placeholder symbols in Dolphin OS/SI/EXI code that may be descriptive scheduler or device globals.
Audit functions still taking EXIChannel versus s32 channel parameters to identify codegen-sensitive signatures.
Look for remaining Pad.c/Pad.h casing references after the lowercase pad rename.
Search for OSMutex.lock or mutexQueue to ensure all users migrated to count and queueMutex.
Compare OSTicksToCalendarTime implementation against later SDK-derived refinements if mismatches remain.
